title: GapReport
description: A gap report: one property's graded verification outcome, written to `<prop>/report.json`.

audit.description: The audit trail: what was checked, the inputs seen, and why the verdict went that way.

gaps.description:
Findings. Empty for a clean full proof; the at-risk property surfaces a `code:"B"` gap
once verified (AC-6).

property.description: The property id — the overlay subdirectory name.

subject.description:
The code under scrutiny, referenced by version — populated once the umbrella is parsed
(AC-5); `null` at the discovery/pending stage.

substrate.description: The verification substrate (backend) — populated once known; `null` when pending.

Audit.description: The audit trail behind a verdict (E3 / G3 — observable, reproducible reasoning).

checked.description: What was checked — the claim/obligation the verdict is about.

inputs.description: The inputs the verdict saw (e.g. the lowering file). Order-stable (G1).

rationale.description: Why the verdict went the way it did.

Gap.description: One finding: a gap between the intended property and what the code guarantees.

code.description: The gap class code (loom's gap taxonomy, e.g. `"B"`).

detail.description: Optional detail — a counterexample, the unproven obligation, etc.

summary.description: A one-line human summary of the gap.

Subject.description: The code under scrutiny, referenced by version — never read at verify time (G1).

path.description: The path to the file under scrutiny, within `repo` at `ref`.

ref.description: The version the subject is pinned at — a tag/ref, referenced not read (G1).

repo.description: The host repository the subject lives in (e.g. `"aiwf"`).

symbol.description: The specific symbol under scrutiny, if narrower than the whole file.

Substrate.description: The verification substrate — the backend that produced the verdict. Grows additively.

oneOf.description: TLA+ / TLC (explicit-state model checking) — the M-0018 second substrate.

Verdict.description: The graded verdict: the rung verification reached, plus operational states.

oneOf.description: Full proof — the property holds unconditionally (top rung).
Bounded proof — the property holds within explicit bounds.
Supported by concrete examples only — no proof (bottom rung).
Verification found the property does not hold, or found a hole (a gap is recorded).
Not yet verified — the schema/seam stage before the backend runs.
The verifier failed to produce a verdict (its nondeterminism isolated + surfaced, G1).
